routineText:
09:00 — Wake up & go to Work
09:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
13:00
13:00 — Black coffee + 1 tsp of sugar

-----------------------
| 14:00 — 🌅 Breakfast
|
| — 5 white of eggs boiled
| — 3 yolk of eggs boiled
| — 5g of olive oil
| — mint tea + 1 tsp of sugar
| — cumin & salt
| — oat 55g + water
| — 1 scoop of protein powder
| — 1 avocado 200g whole
-----------------------

14:00 — return to Work
14:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
18:00
18:00 — Out from work

-----------------------
| 20:30 — ☀️ Lunch (light, pre-football)
| — kefta 90/10 110g
| — khobz complet 35g
-----------------------

22:00 — ⚽ Football training (1h30)

23:30 — 4 dates + water (post-training)

-----------------------
| 23:45 — 🌙 Post-match Dinner
| — kefta 90/10 140g (remainder, deferred from lunch)
| — 300g legume sauté (carrot, courgette, onion)
| — 300g potatoes (boiled)
| — 1 scoop of protein powder
| — 1 perly nature
| — 2g Almonds
| — 2g Walnuts
| — 2g Cashews
| — 2g Pistachios
| — 1 kiwi
-----------------------

01:00 — Sleep

adjustments.reason: Bread used twice in a day — protocol rule for gas. Khobz at lunch stays; breakfast bread goes.
Adds back the missing 77 kcal / 13g carbs from removing the bread — keeps macros balanced.

adjustments.from: Kefta 250g + 300g légumes sautés all at 20:30 lunch, 1h30 before football
adjustments.to: Kefta split 110g (pre-match lunch) / 140g (post-match dinner) — légumes 300g moved entirely to post-match
adjustments.reason: A full fatty (kefta) + fibrous (légumes) meal 1h30 before an intense football session risks cramping and gas mid-match. Lighter, faster-digesting lunch now; the rest becomes the post-match recovery meal.

adjustments.from: Post-match dinner short by ~570 kcal / 133g carbs of the training target
adjustments.to: Added 300g potatoes to dinner; 4 dates + water already logged post-training
adjustments.reason: Dates right after the match were a good fast-carb move for glycogen refill. Potatoes at dinner (clean carb, low fat, no gas-list ingredient) close most of the remaining training-day carb/calorie gap without overloading fat or fiber further.

verdict.text_html: Solid training-day recovery — 2,030 kcal (−270 vs training target). Dates + potatoes closed most of the carb gap; protein and fat right on point.

coverage.name: Calories
coverage.note: 270 kcal short — dates + potatoes closed most of the rest-day-sized gap

coverage.name: Protein
coverage.note: Kefta, whey ×2, eggs, perly — right at target

coverage.name: Carbs
coverage.note: Up from 117g pre-dinner-redesign — dates (24g) + potatoes 300g (52.5g) did the work

coverage.name: Fat
coverage.note: Unchanged — avocado, kefta, yolks, olive oil, perly stack

coverage.name: Fiber
coverage.note: Potatoes + légumes + oat push slightly over — fine for a training day

coverage.name: Gas / bloat risk
coverage.note: Lunch stayed light pre-match; dinner is a big post-training plate but potatoes/kefta/veg aren't gas-list items

coverage.name: Protocol adherence
coverage.note: Dinner at 23:45 (post-match) breaks the dinner-by-22h rule — unavoidable with a 22h game; cumin ✓, single bread ✓

coverage.name: Vitamin A
coverage.note: Unchanged — carrot remains the dominant Vit A source

coverage.name: Potassium
coverage.note: Big jump from 36% — potatoes (~1275mg) + dates (~210mg) on top of avocado/kiwi/veg

good: Dates + water right after the match — smart fast-carb refuel before the bigger meal
Potatoes at dinner closed most of the carb/calorie gap without adding fat or gas-list ingredients
Protein (98%) and fat (109%) both right on target
Potassium jumped from 36% to 68% of target — potatoes + dates did real work there
bad: Still ~270 kcal / 61g carbs short of a fully-fueled training day
Dinner lands at 23:45 (post-match) — breaks the dinner-by-22h rule, though the 22h game leaves no earlier option
Omega-3 still minimal — no fatty fish day (structural gap)
Magnesium at ~70% — better than before (49%) but still under target
loggedItems: Whole wheat bread — removed (bread used twice, gas risk)
300g potatoes, boiled (added to close the training-day carb gap)

fullTable.note: Kefta, whey ×2, eggs, perly — right at target
3 × 1L blocks from schedule, plus water with the post-training dates
Potatoes (+6.3g) and dates (+2.6g) push it slightly over — fine
Unchanged — walnuts 2g only (0.13g ALA), no fatty fish
Unchanged — structural gap without fatty fish
Potatoes + dates add ~2.7mg — close to target now
Unchanged — kefta + egg yolks
Potatoes (+69mg) and dates (+17mg) close a lot of this gap
Unchanged — perly + whey are the main sources
Unchanged — kefta covers the target
Potatoes (~1,275mg) + dates (~210mg) nearly double the previous coverage
Potatoes add ~54µg on top of avocado + yolks + carrot
Potatoes add ~59mg on top of kiwi + vegetables
Unchanged — carrot + egg yolks
Unchanged — structural gap
Unchanged — avocado + olive oil + yolks
Unchanged — kiwi + avocado
Potatoes + dates add ~1mg on top of kefta + avocado
Unchanged — eggs + kefta exceptional
Potatoes add ~0.24mg — closer to target now
Unchanged — egg whites + kefta
Potatoes + dates add ~3.7mg — right at target now
Potatoes + dates push this over target for the first time
Potatoes add ~36mg on top of egg yolks (dominant)
Fromage frais mild — consider swapping for raib for better gut value
Potatoes add a little — still within normal range
Unchanged — carrot, kiwi, avocado, oat, potato skin
Potatoes add ~171mg on top of whey + kefta + yolks — already well above
Potatoes + dates close most of this gap — close to target now
Unchanged — oat remains the main grain source
Unchanged — egg yolks + avocado + kefta
Unchanged — 3 egg yolks provide 75µg
